lines.text: Originally written by Alexa Junge
Monica and Rachel's apartment. Rachel is on the phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mom, would you relax. That was 10 blocks from here and, the, the woman was walking alone at night, I would never do that. Mom, c'mon, stop worrying. This is a safe street, this is a safe building, there's nothing [a pigeon flies in the window and lands on the table] OH MY GOOOD, oh my God, oh I gotta go, I gotta go, I gotta go. [hangs up] OK, that's fine, you just read the paper, I'm gonna get a pot, it's not for you. [grabs a pot and lid] OK, that's fine, read the Family Circus, enjoy the gentle comedy. [puts pot over the pigeon] Aaahh, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, aaaaahh. [knock at the door] It's open you guys.
[a stranger enters with flowers]

lines.speaker: Stranger
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, hi can I help you?

lines.speaker: Stranger
lines.text: Yeah, I'm looking for Phoebe, does she still live here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, no she doesn't but I can, I can get a message to her.

lines.speaker: Stranger
lines.text: Great. Uhh, just tell her her husband stopped by. [leaves flowers on bar]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? [in surprise she forgets she has the pigeon in the pot and lets it get away]

lines.speaker: Stranger
lines.text: Hey, how, how did you do that?
OPENING TITLES
Monica and Rachels apartment. The whole gang is there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is unbelievable Phoebs, how can you be married?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I mean, I'm not married married, ya know, he's just a friend and he's gay and he's just from Canada and he just needed a green card.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe you married Duncan. I mean how could you not tell me? We lived together, we told each other everything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry Monica but I knew if I told you, you'd get really, like, judgemental and you would not approve.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course I wouldn't approve, I mean, you were totally in love with this guy who, hello, was gay. I mean, what the hell were you thinking?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You see, and you thought she'd be judgemental.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, I wasn't in love with him and I was just helping out a friend.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please, when he left town you stayed in your pajamas for a month and I saw you eat a cheeseburger.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Huuh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I might have.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe you didn't tell me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, c'mon, like you tell me everything.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What have I not told you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. Umm, how about the fact that the underwear out there on the telephone pole is yours from when you were having sex with Fun Bobby out on the terrace.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, who told you? [turns to Chandler who's looking sheepish] You are dead meat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't know it was a big secret.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh it's not big, not at all, you know, kinda the same lines as, say, oh I don't know, having a third nipple.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You have a third nipple?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You bitch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whip it out, whip it out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: C'mon, there's nothin' to see, it's just a tiny bump, it's totally useless.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh as, as opposed to your other multi-functional nipples?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't believe you. You told me it was a nubbin.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, what did you think a nubbin was?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, you see somethin', you hear a word, I thought that's what it was. Let me see it again.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah, show it. Show it. The nubbin, the nubbin, the nubbin.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey was in a porno movie.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Huuh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If I'm goin' down, I'm takin' everybody with me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You were in a porno?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh, alright, alright, alright, I was young and I just wanted a job, OK. But at the last minute I couldn't go through with it so they let me be the guy who comes in to fix the copier but can't 'cause there's people havin' sex on it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is wild.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [to Chandler] So what's it shaped like?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, is there a hair on it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What happens if you flick it?
Central Perk. Chandler, Ross, and Julie are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, uh, does it do anything, you know, special?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why yes Ross, pressing my third nipple opens the delivery entrance to the magical land of Narnia.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: You know, in some cultures having a third nipple is actually a sign of virility. You get the best huts and women dance naked around you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh? Are, uh, any of these cultures, per chance, in the tri-state area?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, you are so amazing, is there anything you, you don't know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: [to Monica at the counter] Ooh, Julie's so smart, Julie's so special.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look honey, I wanted you to hook up with Ross as much as you did. But he's with her now and you're just gonna have to get over it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, I'm gonna have to get over it. God, see I didn't know that's I had to do, I just have to get over it.
[Phoebe enters all dressed up]

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Woah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Foxy lady.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Where you goin'?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, I'm gonna go meet Duncan, he's skating tonight at the Garden, he's in the Capades.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The Ice Capades?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no the gravel capades. Yeah, the turns aren't as fast but when Snoopy falls. . . funny.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe you're dressing up for him. I mean, you're just, you're setting yourself up all over again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK, no. For your information I'm going to see him so I can put all those feeling behind me. OK, and the reason I'm dressed like this is because I think it's nice to look nice for your gay husband.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [holding cream pitcher] Oh, darnit, we're all out of milk. [holds pitcher in front of Chandler's chest and flips the lid] Hey Chandler, would you fill me up here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh I see, I see, because of the third nipple thing. Ha ha ha ha. . .
Central Perk close to closing. Ross and Julie are still there. Rachel is cleaning tables.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK sweetie, I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: See you later Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye-bye Julie. [Julie leaves]
[Rachel is still cleaning, Ross is laying on the couch. Ross kicks Rachel in the butt.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.
[Ross kicks her again]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, c'mon, cut it out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can I ask you somethin'?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Naa.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? C'mon, talk to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK, what's the longest you've been in the relationship before ha, have, having the sex?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? Who's not having. . . Are you and Julie not, are, are you and, are you and Julie not having sex?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Technically, huh, no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow. Is it, is it 'cause she's so cold in bed. Or, or is it 'cause she's like, kinda bossy, makes it feel like school?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, she's great and it's not like we haven't done anything. I mean, uh, uh, we, we do plenty of other stuff, lot's of other stuff, like uhh. . .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no no no, don't need to know the details.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's just, it's, it's me. You, you know I've only been with one woman my whole life and she turned out to be a lesbian. So now I've got myself all psyched out, you know, and it's become, like this, this thing and I. . . Well, you just must think I'm weird.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, no, no I don't think it's weird, I think, I think umm, in fact, in fact you know what I think?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think it's sexy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sexy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let me tell you something. As a woman there is nothing sexier than a man who does not want to have sex.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No kidding?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. In fact you know what I'd do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'd wait.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You'd wait?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, absolutely. I would wait and wait. . . then I'd wait some more.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, I don't care how much she tells you she wants it, I don't care if begs, she pleads, she tells you she, she's gonna have sex with, with another man. That just means it's working.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Women really want this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: More than jewelry. [Rachel struts off, extremely pleased with herself]
Madison Square Garden. Duncan's dressing room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ta-da.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Ahh, look at you, you look great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do I? Thank you, so do you.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sparkly. So, wow, this is pretty wonerful, huh. Mr. major capades guy. I, I remember when you were just, like, King Friday in Mr. Roger's Ice is Nice.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: You always said I'd make it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, well, ya know, I'm kind of spooky that way. Wooo.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: I missed you. [they hug] I'm gonna get changed.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Um, now. Phoebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, right, OK. Ole.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, the matador. [Duncan leaves] Ole, ha ha ha.
Ross's apartment. Ross and Julie are setting the table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Julie, can you hold this for a second, thanks. [hands her a bowl and kisses her]
[Chandler, Monica, Joey, and Rachel enter. Ross and Julie don't notice.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, Julie.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry, you had a paleontologist on your face. But, uh, it's gone now, you're alright.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi everyone.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [pulls Rachel aside] I just, I wanted to thank you for our uh, our little talk before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God, no problem. So you're gonna go with the uh, waiting thing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I was going to, but after I talked to you, I talked to Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What did, what did he say?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Basically he told me to get over myself and just do it, ya know. So I though about what you said and I though about what he said and, well, his way I get to have sex tonight so. . .
Ross's apartment. After dinner. Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's this in my pocket? Why it's Joey's porno movie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pop it in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm fine with it, I mean, if you're OK watching a video filled with two nippled people. [Chandler puts the tape in]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great, people having sex, that's just what I need to see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's wrong with people having sex?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, well um, you know, these movies are offensive and uh, degrading to women and females. And uh, and the lighting's always unflattering. And, Monica help me out here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hell, I wanna see Joey.
[video starts with the cheesy porn disco music]

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: So is there like a story or do they just stard doing it right. . . oh, never mind.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OK, now wait a minute. That is the craziest typing test I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All I say is, she better get the job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Looks to me like he's the one getting the job.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Shh, OK, here I come, here I come. See I'm comin' to fix the copier, I can't get to the copier, I'm thinkin' what do I do, what do I do. . . so I just watch 'em have sex. And then I say, wait, here's my line, [Joey from TV] you know that's bad fo r the paper tray.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nice work my friend.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you. Wait wait wait wait, you see me again. Hang on, the guy's butt's blockin' me. There I am, there I am, there I am, there I am, there I am. . .
Madison Square Garden, Duncan's dressing room. Phoebe is fixing her hair and Duncan enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So um, so what's up, you came to see me yesterday.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Oh, yeah, um, alright, I kinda need a divorce.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh. . .K. How come?

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Umm, actually, I'm getting married again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Oh God, I don't know how to tell you this. I'm straight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huuh.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I, I don't, I don't understand, how can you be straight? I mean, you're, you're so smart and funny and you throw such great Academy Award parties.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: I know, that's what I kept telling myself but you just reach a point where you can't live a lie anymore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So how long have you known?

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Well I guess on some level I always knew I was straight. I though I was supposed to be something else, you know, I'm an ice dancer, all my friends are gay, I was just tryin' to fit in.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And um, and there's actually a, a woman?

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Her name's Debra.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Well is she, is she the first that you've been with?

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: Well, I've never told you this but, there were one or two times, back in college, when I'd get really drunk, go to a straight bar and wake up with a woman next to me. But I, I, I told myself it was the liquor and e-everyone experiments in college.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: But now I know I don't have a choice about this, I was born this way.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I, I don't know what to say. I mean, you know, you're married to someone for six years and you think you know him and then one day says, 'Oh, I'm not gay.'

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: I'm, I'm still me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why couldn't you have just figured this out six years ago?
Ross's apartment. Everyone is sitting around. Monica enters from bathroom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know, it still smells like monkey in there.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: That saves us a conversation.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, listen, this has been great but I'm officially wiped.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too, we should get goin'.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, I mean, no, c'mon you guys, I mean, c'mon look it's only eleven thirty. Let's just talk, we never just hang out and talk anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, that's all we do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe that's all we do, what about Julie?

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: What about Julie?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you have been in our lives for nearly two months now and we don't really know you. I mean, who is Julie? I mean, what do you like, what don't you like? We wanna hear everything.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Well, that could take a while.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So. I mean, who here does not have the time to get to know Julie?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got the time to get to know Julie.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I got time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, I know her pretty well, can I go? [Rachel gives her a look from hell] That's fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK Julie, so now let's start with your childhood, what was that like?

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Well, in a nutshell. . .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nah, uh, uh, uh, uh.
Madison Square Garden, Duncan's dressing room. Phoebe is signing the divorce papers.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, um, have you told your parents?

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: No, but it'll be OK, they're pretty cool, my brother's straight so. . .

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: [handing him the papers] Here you go. You know what, I just have one more question, um, if you had figured this out sooner and um, I had been around, do you think that I would have been the one who. . . no, um, I'm sorry, don't tell me, I don't th ink either answer would make me feel better.

lines.speaker: Duncan
lines.text: I love you Phoebe. [they hug and kiss]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So your brother's straigh huh? Seriously.
Ross's apartment. Julie is telling her live story.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: And my second grade teacher was Ms. Thomas, and my first grade teacher was Mrs. Cobb.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mrs., Mrs. Gobb?

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: No Cobb, as in cobb salad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now, what exactly is in a cobb salad?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm goin' home.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?
[Outside in the hallway, Chandler, Joey, and Monica exit]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Boy that Julie's a talker, huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Goodnight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, it's pretty late, you're probably uh, not still planning on. . .

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no no, I am.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, are hey, are you nervous?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, no, I uh, I have done it before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, OK, I mean uh, what, how are you gonna handle it. I mean, are, are ya gonna, are ya gonna talk about it before hand, are you just gonna pounce?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I uh, I don't know, I guess I'm just gonna see, see what happens.

lines.speaker: Rahcel
lines.text: OK, gook luck.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha, uhh, what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing, I mean, um, it is your first time with her and, you know if the first time doesn't go well, well then that's, that's pretty darn hard to recover from.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK, now I'm nervous.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe you should put it off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, I don't wanna put it off, I just, God I just, I spent last year being so unbelievably miserable, ya know, and now, now I'm actually happy. You know, I mean, really happy. I just, I just don't wanna, I don't wanna mess it up, ya know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, yeah, sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, it's not your fault.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe it, maybe it doesn't have to be this tough. I mean, maybe you were on the right track with this whole, you know, spontaneous thing. I mean, women really like that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I mean, you know it, I mean, if it were me I, I, you know, I'd want you to, I don't know, like catch me off guard, you know, with like a really good kiss, you know really, sort of um, soft at first, then maybe um brush the hair away from my face, and look far into my eyes in a way that let's me know that something amazing is about to happen.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [being drawn in by her talk] Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And then, I don't know, I mean you'd pull me really close to you so that, so that I'd be pressed up, you know, right against you. And, um, it would get kind of sweaty and uh, and blurry, and then it's just happening.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh. . . Thanks Rach, goodnight. [goes back in apartment]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, God.
CLOSING CREDITS
The next morning on the street. Ross is dancing along, Singing in the Rain is playing. Two old ladies are sitting on a bench.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Old Woman
lines.text: Well, somebody got some last night.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Twice.
END
